title: Review & setlist: It was 100 degrees in Boston, and Goose was on fire
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Goose delivered a high-energy performance at Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 1, 2026, despite 100-degree heat.
Setlist included mostly older hits with only one song from their recent album 'Big Modern!', maintaining their jam band roots.
The concert featured standout moments like 'Electric Avenue' and concluded with their first performance of 'Doobie Song' in a year.

title: Everything to know about July 4 in Boston (including how to watch the fireworks)
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: The 2026 Boston Pops Fireworks Spectacular at the DCR Hatch Shell starts at 7 p.m. with fireworks at 9:15 p.m.
WHHD-TV (Channel 7) will broadcast the event locally, and it can be streamed on CNN.com and the CNN app.
The event features Lainey Wilson, Chance The Rapper, Trombone Shorty, Megan Hilty, and is hosted by Jane Lynch.

title: In new podcast, former North Andover police officer Kelsey Fitzsimmons calls first judge ‘evil’ and recounts being shot
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover police officer acquitted of assault, starts a podcast detailing her experience.
Fitzsimmons discusses her struggle with postpartum depression and recounts being shot by a colleague.
She criticizes Judge Kathleen McCarthy-Neyman, calling her 'evil' for decisions made during her legal proceedings.

title: Bruins’ breakout prospect Cooper Simpson has a clear path to follow in Will Zellers
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Cooper Simpson and Will Zellers, both coming from Minnesota, are Boston Bruins prospects preparing for the Fighting Hawks.
Simpson was a third-round pick in the 2025 NHL Draft, scoring 34 goals with the Youngstown Phantoms in 2025-26.
Zellers, part of the 2025 Charlie Coyle trade, led the USHL in goals in 2024-25 and has progressed rapidly.

title: New York woman indicted in alleged sex trafficking operation out of apartments in Allston, Brighton
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Zengzeng Liu, 40, from New York, indicted for running an international sex trafficking ring in Allston and Brighton, MA.
Liu allegedly recruited women from Asia for commercial sex acts and operated brothels using fraudulent documents.
Authorities found nearly $105,000 in cash and evidence of sex trafficking during searches of two apartments.

title: ‘As a former Celtic, I do not approve’: Basketball world reacts to the Jaylen Brown trade
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Celtics traded Jaylen Brown to the 76ers for Paul George and four draft picks, shocking the basketball world.
Former Celtics player Kendrick Perkins disapproves of the trade, while others highlight Paul George's health concerns.
Local and national media criticized the trade, questioning the Celtics' strategy and potential lack of improvements.

title: A conversation with David Ortiz about the 2004 Red Sox, life as a baseball dad, and the current state of the team
author: Trevor Hass
bullets: David Ortiz engaged with over 1,000 fans at a Fanatics meet-and-greet in Boston.
Ortiz reminisced about the teamwork and chemistry that led the 2004 Red Sox to a World Series win.
Ortiz expressed optimism about the Red Sox, encouraging patience for the team's future success.

title: A 1700s Colonial reproduction in Western Massachusetts — can you guess the price?
author: Emily Turner
bullets: The Colonial reproduction house in Worthington was built in 1976 and offers 1,889 square feet.
The property features 3 bedrooms, 3 baths, and has private sewer and water systems.
The 2025 property taxes for the house amount to $8,011.

title: UMass University Campus nurses vote to authorize 14-day strike
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: UMass Memorial Medical Center nurses voted 1,233 to 17 to authorize a potential 14-day strike.
The vote aims to secure a fair contract focusing on patient care, safety, and nurse recruitment and retention.
The strike authorization allows flexibility for future action if contract negotiations remain unresolved.

title: 5 best new restaurant openings and dining updates in Boston (July 2, 2026)
author: Nathan Tavares
bullets: The Alley food hall opened in June at One Post Office Square, featuring eight dining concepts over 6,500 square feet.
Flann O'Brien's in Mission Hill is set to reopen at the end of 2026 with Irish-American comfort food and weekend brunch.
Ye Olde Pepper Candy Companie launched a candy popup at Old North Church, offering historic confections daily until Halloween.
